edges.description: TP53-mutant secretory cells acquire a proliferative intraepithelial phenotype.
edges.source: TP53 Mutation in Tubal Secretory Cells

edges.description: STIC lesions accumulate further genomic instability, often via HRR defects.
Genomic instability promotes invasion and early transcoelomic dissemination.
edges.target: Invasive High-Grade Serous Carcinoma and Peritoneal Spread

evidence.reference_title: Maintenance Olaparib in Patients with Newly Diagnosed Advanced Ovarian Cancer.
evidence.snippet: high-grade serous or endometrioid ovarian cancer, primary peritoneal cancer, or fallopian-tube cancer (or a combination thereof) with a mutation in BRCA1, BRCA2, or both

evidence.reference_title: Maintenance Olaparib in Patients with Newly Diagnosed Advanced Ovarian Cancer.
evidence.snippet: high-grade serous or endometrioid ovarian cancer, primary peritoneal cancer, or fallopian-tube cancer (or a combination thereof) with a mutation in BRCA1, BRCA2, or both

nodes.description: Loss of homologous recombination repair, most often through germline or somatic BRCA1/BRCA2 mutation or BRCA1 promoter methylation, drives genomic instability, widespread copy-number alteration, and platinum/PARP-inhibitor sensitivity in high-grade serous carcinoma.

biological_processes: double-strand break repair via homologous recombination

evidence.reference_title: Integrated genomic analyses of ovarian carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Pathway analyses suggested that homologous recombination is defective in about half of the tumours analysed

nodes.description: Invasive high-grade serous carcinoma sheds from the fimbria and disseminates transcoelomically across the peritoneal cavity and omentum, producing the advanced-stage presentation typical of tubo-ovarian high-grade serous carcinoma.
nodes.id: Invasive High-Grade Serous Carcinoma and Peritoneal Spread

evidence.reference_title: The genomic trajectory of ovarian high-grade serous carcinoma can be observed in STIC lesions.
evidence.snippet: which in turn progress into invasive HGSC, which readily spreads to the ovary and disseminates around the peritoneal cavity

nodes.description: TP53-mutant secretory cells in the fimbria expand into serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma, a non-invasive intraepithelial malignancy with a p53 immunostaining signature and elevated proliferation, recognized as the direct precursor of high-grade serous carcinoma.

evidence.reference_title: The genomic trajectory of ovarian high-grade serous carcinoma can be observed in STIC lesions.
evidence.snippet: p53 signatures evolve into serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC) lesions, which in turn progress into invasive HGSC

nodes.description: Near-universal somatic TP53 mutation in secretory epithelial cells of the distal fallopian tube is the earliest and defining molecular event, producing the "p53 signature" in morphologically benign epithelium that is the precursor of STIC.
nodes.id: TP53 Mutation in Tubal Secretory Cells

evidence.reference_title: The genomic trajectory of ovarian high-grade serous carcinoma can be observed in STIC lesions.
evidence.snippet: Ovarian high-grade serous carcinoma (HGSC) originates in the fallopian tube, with secretory cells carrying a TP53 mutation, known as p53 signatures, identified as potential precursors.
